ops.text: repo-bootstrap `verify --layer go` runs bare `go test -race ./...` internally — on daemonkit (and any repo carrying the fork-bomb harness rule) that's exactly the banned invocation once `proc.Spawn` self-exec tests land in P1. Safe today (no spawn code yet), but the P5 repo-bootstrap daemonkit layer must ship a harness-aware verify (route the test step through scripts/test.sh) or scaffolded consumers will casually fork-bomb themselves at verify time. Also: `verify --layer go` hard-FAILs on the skill's own sanctioned "library, not a CLI" escape hatch (no cmd/ → "binary smoke test: no cmd/<name>/ directory found") — the hatch deserves a --library flag instead of a documented-but-red check.
